summary: Code skeptic review found the json-render side-car design is directionally plausible but not proven. Multi-render portability, Ink 40/60 layout, OpenCode side-car transport, schema-kernel migration scope, and Clean Architecture boundaries all remain high-risk proof gates rather than validated implementation facts.
must_fix_before_merge: Prove a domain envelope distinct from renderer specs
Prove Ink and React adapters against the same hm-settings slice
Prove OpenCode attach/event failure handling and auth assumptions
Test 40/60 layout behavior and fallback thresholds in terminals
blocked_routes: Implementing side-car rendering as if the current ADR claims are already proven
Moving HmSettingDashboardProof into schema-kernel without first defining additive dashboard envelope contracts
Assuming createOpencodeClient alone validates side-car runtime connectivity
recommended_next_action: Run Phase 0 proof gates before implementation: contract extraction spike, dual-adapter prototype, OpenCode live attach/event test, and terminal-width layout tests.
